rationale: Both runs satisfy the major objective checks, correctly identify the two seeded mechanisms (lexicographic seq ordering and adjust resurrecting missing items), preserve append-only behavior, add undo, and keep tests passing without dependencies. Run A is stronger overall: its undo design uses self-contained compensation payloads, its evaluator ran additional adversarial probes beyond the shipped tests, and its evidence explicitly covers edge cases such as no-op historical adjust undo, interleaved undo exhaustion, unremove after unadjust, history preservation, golden checksum stability, and log-prefix append-only behavior. Run B is also good, but its marker-only logical-skip undo has a documented semantic wart around hand-built historical no-op adjusts, and its process is more self-congratulatory with less independent edge probing despite assigning itself perfect completeness/evidence scores. A has the better final-product confidence and the better harness-process evidence.
